sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 55

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 55:1
items.html: The place of the Zivug [coupling] — that is, where the reality of the Masach [screen] and the Aviut [coarseness] that raises Returning Light is found — is called Peh [mouth] or Nekev [aperture]: in the Rosh [head] it is called Peh, and when it is in the Guf [body] it is called Nekev. The reason is that the place of the Masach is truly the discernment of the ending of the force of Tzimtzum [restriction], which ends upon the Light and does not let it pass through it and spread below it, as is known; rather, by virtue of the Returning Light in it, it spreads from above downward into the discernment of Toch and Sof, at whose end that discernment of ending found in the Masach at the place of the Zivug is revealed, as is known. Thus, although the discernment of the Masach that raises Returning Light is a complete ending force upon the upper Lights, it nevertheless passes some measure of the Lights through to the discernments of the ten Sefirot of the Guf. And this is likened to an aperture and hole made within the force of boundary and ending in the Masach, where its force was annulled, and the Lights pass through there freely at their will; therefore the place of the Zivug is titled Nekev, being an open way within the Masach that returns the Lights back. Understand this well. And it is the Peh of the Rosh in the Partzuf of Gadlut; and it is the Pi HaChazeh [opening of the chest] in the Partzuf of the Katnut of Yenika [suckling], whose HGT are its Rosh, so its Peh is found at the place of the Chazeh; and it is the Pi HaTabur [opening of the navel] in the Partzuf of the embryo, whose head is between its knees, having not even a discernment of a Rosh of HGT, so its Peh is found at the place of the Tabur. However, this Zivug of the Tabur is not a Zivug of its own, but in the discernment of incorporation in the Masach of Ima; and this is the secret of its eating there only what its mother eats, for it does not yet have a discernment of a particular Zivug of its own, resembling the Peh of the Rosh.

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Topics 55:2
items.html: (to the question) And it is known that when ZA [Zeir Anpin] imparts Mochin of Katnut to the Nukva, he awakens and renews his own discernment of the Masach of Katnut, through which he himself received the Mochin of Katnut at the time of his own Katnut. It follows that he must impart to the Nukva from the discernment of the Yesod of Katnut in him; but this Yesod has already risen to the Chazeh [chest] and become the discernment of Tiferet, and all the force of the Hey Tachtona [lower Hey] has already descended from it, after it attained the new Achoraim [posterior], as is known. If so, there is now no aperture at all in his Chazeh — for the old HGT, in which the aperture of the Chazeh was, rose and became the Peh of the Rosh, since these HGT became HBD, and now the Yesod stands there in the upper third of Tiferet, and there is no aperture there at all. Whence, then, can ZA awaken his Peh of Katnut so as to impart the corresponding discernment to the Nukva? This is what the Rav says: the Butzina de-Kardinuta [dark flame] — which is the point of Malchut that was cleaving to him in the Yesod of Katnut, and even now has not moved from there, but is in him without action or recognition, since the force of Tzimtzum had already descended from the Eynaim [eyes], as above — now returns and renews her force in the discernment of her Hey Tachtona, as it was at the time of her Katnut; and thereby a place of Zivug is made anew at the Peh of the Chazeh, in order to measure from there the Mochin of Katnut for the Nukva. And this is what he says — that the Butzina de-Kardinuta at the place of the Chazeh of ZA makes an aperture at the place of the Chazeh from behind, that is, a place of Zivug anew; but this place of Zivug is discerned as his discernment of Achoraim, for it is not for his own need, and his own discernment of the Light of the Panim [face] is not diminished at all because of it. Hence it is discerned that in the Panim of the Chazeh — that is, in his own discernment — there is no renewal of an aperture at all, and the whole renewed aperture is made behind the Chazeh, intentionally for the sake of the Nukva. And you find here that although all the Nukva's cleaving is only in the new NHY [Netzah, Hod, Yesod] of the Gadlut alone, as above in Answer 158, she nevertheless cannot receive from the discernment of these NHY except only from the old NHY that rose to HGT, whose bestowal passes to her through the aperture of the Chazeh. And remember this. (p. 777, Item 67)
